title: Helianthemum teretifolium
intro: Helianthemum teretifolium, also known as silver rockrose, is a perennial shrubby plant belonging to the family Cistaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions, such as wound healing, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Helianthemum teretifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are considered effective preparations for harnessing its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Helianthemum teretifolium is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cistaceae. It is native to North America and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. The plant is classified as a shrub, with a low, spreading growth habit. Helianthemum teretifolium is a member of the genus Helianthemum, which is commonly known as rockrose. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and adaptations to its arid environment.
uses: Helianthemum teretifolium has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and flowers are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating infections and promoting healing. Additionally, it may have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. More research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

preparations: Helianthemum teretifolium, also known as dune rockrose, has been used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties.
